item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think it's really the actual number of logs in my opinion so much as it is their narrative purpose.

I think this is it. I'd feel the same way by the end of the piece if there were only one of these logs because there was no escalation to them. This article isn't particularly long as it is, but it felt like wasted word count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think it's really the actual number of logs in my opinion so much as it is their narrative purpose. I think all five logs more or less accomplish the same thing in the scope of the piece-expanding the feeling of weirdness/body horror, and I would argue that that makes most of them redundant.

Five logs is totally fine if they dramatically expand the story/push it forward. 6, 7, even 8 logs would also be fine if they could do the same. In this particular case though, I don't get a sense that they do much that's all that different from each other. As a result, it just feels a little bloated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There were only 5 logs, I didn't think that was that many?? anyway, I'm sorry you didn't like it, but thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not bad. The writing is solid, but the story itself was just eh. I think the number of logs hurt it in my opinion, simply because there are so many of them, and they don't seem different enough narratively speaking to justify how many of them there are, or their length. Additionally, the core premise isn't super new territory, nor does it do much with the concept that feels exciting.

The reveal of the backstory at the end of the piece feels like a "and here's why you should care!" sort of slapped together to post-facto us into the emotional reveal, but it's done so abruptly and all at the end in such a way that it doesn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stuff doesn't usually disturb me, but the corpse descriptions absolutely did. The body horror language was especially on-point.

Hard +1

item.title: Re: Author Post - Malyce
item.content:encoded: It was great fun working with you, Mew! Thank you for giving me the opportunity.

item.title: Author Post - Mew
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to DrAkimoto , cybersqyd , and Cerastes for taking a look and critiquing this!

I had a blast working with MalyceGraves on this! From conceptualizing this to working out the details, Mal was so helpful and awesome. They really made this piece stand out.

Till next time!
